metadata.description: Mathlib coverage analysis for Sutherland Number Theory Lecture 2

Lecture2/02_00a_Discussion.notes: Localization S⁻¹A: definition, universal property, construction all in Mathlib.

mathlib_declarations.name: Localization
mathlib_declarations.description: S⁻¹A as a type

mathlib_declarations.name: IsLocalization
mathlib_declarations.description: Typeclass for localization

mathlib_declarations.name: Localization.mk
mathlib_declarations.description: Constructor a/s for localization elements

mathlib_declarations.name: IsLocalization.lift
mathlib_declarations.description: Universal property of localization

mathlib_declarations.name: IsFractionRing
mathlib_declarations.description: Fraction field as localization at nonZeroDivisors

mathlib_declarations.name: FractionRing
mathlib_declarations.description: Concrete fraction field construction

Lecture2/02_00b_Discussion.notes: Ideal extension/contraction under localization; b = b^{ce} for localizations.

mathlib_declarations.name: Ideal.map
mathlib_declarations.description: Extension of an ideal under a ring homomorphism

mathlib_declarations.name: Ideal.comap
mathlib_declarations.description: Contraction of an ideal under a ring homomorphism

mathlib_declarations.name: IsLocalization.map_comap
mathlib_declarations.description: b^{ce} = b for localizations (extension-contraction identity)

Lecture2/02_01_Theorem.notes: Theorem 2.1 (prime ideal correspondence under localization) is fully in Mathlib as an order isomorphism.

mathlib_declarations.name: IsLocalization.orderIsoOfPrime
mathlib_declarations.description: Order isomorphism between primes of S⁻¹A and primes of A disjoint from S

Lecture2/02_02_Remark.notes: Noetherian and PID preserved under localization, ideal generation in localizations.

mathlib_declarations.name: IsLocalization.isNoetherianRing
mathlib_declarations.description: Localization of noetherian ring is noetherian

mathlib_declarations.name: Localization.AtPrime.isPrincipalIdealRing
mathlib_declarations.description: Localization of PID at prime is PID

Lecture2/02_02a_Discussion.notes: Localization at prime A_p, local ring structure, unique maximal ideal.

mathlib_declarations.name: Localization.AtPrime
mathlib_declarations.description: Localization at a prime ideal A_p

mathlib_declarations.name: IsLocalization.AtPrime
mathlib_declarations.description: Typeclass for localization at a prime

mathlib_declarations.name: Localization.AtPrime.instIsLocalRing
mathlib_declarations.description: A_p is a local ring

mathlib_declarations.name: IsLocalRing
mathlib_declarations.description: Local ring typeclass (unique maximal ideal)

mathlib_declarations.name: IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal
mathlib_declarations.description: The unique maximal ideal of a local ring

Lecture2/02_03_Remark.notes: Warning about ambiguity of a/b notation in localizations. Pedagogical remark, not formalizable as a standalone result.

Lecture2/02_04_Example.notes: k[x] localized at (x-2) is a DVR. Mathlib has DVR definition and polynomial rings. The specific example would need to be constructed.

mathlib_declarations.name: IsDiscreteValuationRing
mathlib_declarations.description: DVR typeclass

mathlib_declarations.name: Polynomial
mathlib_declarations.description: Polynomial ring k[x]

mathlib_declarations.name: Localization.AtPrime
mathlib_declarations.description: Localization at prime for building k[x]_(x-2)

Lecture2/02_05_Example.notes: Z_(p) is a DVR with p-adic valuation. Mathlib has padic valuation infrastructure but the specific localization-DVR example needs assembly.

mathlib_declarations.name: IsDiscreteValuationRing
mathlib_declarations.description: DVR typeclass

mathlib_declarations.name: padicValNat
mathlib_declarations.description: p-adic valuation on natural numbers

mathlib_declarations.name: ZMod
mathlib_declarations.description: Z/pZ (finite fields F_p)

Lecture2/02_05a_Discussion.notes: Module localization S⁻¹M, tensor product equivalence S⁻¹M ≅ M ⊗_A S⁻¹A.

mathlib_declarations.name: LocalizedModule
mathlib_declarations.description: S⁻¹M as a type

mathlib_declarations.name: IsLocalizedModule
mathlib_declarations.description: Typeclass for module localization

mathlib_declarations.name: LocalizedModule.mk
mathlib_declarations.description: Constructor m/s for localized module elements

mathlib_declarations.name: LocalizedModule.equivTensorProduct
mathlib_declarations.description: S⁻¹M ≃ₗ M ⊗_A S⁻¹A

Lecture2/02_06_Proposition.notes: Proposition 2.6 (M = ∩ M_p over all primes). Mathlib has related results for ideals in Dedekind domains but the general submodule-of-vector-space version may need custom proof.

mathlib_declarations.name: Submodule.iInf_localization_eq_bot (Maximal)
mathlib_declarations.description: Related intersection-of-localizations results for Dedekind domains

Lecture2/02_06a_Discussion.notes: Localization of an ideal as module = extension of ideal. Mathlib has both concepts but the explicit identification I_p = IA_p may need assembly.

mathlib_declarations.name: Ideal.map
mathlib_declarations.description: Ideal extension IA_p

mathlib_declarations.name: LocalizedModule
mathlib_declarations.description: Module localization I_p

Lecture2/02_07_Corollary.notes: Corollary 2.7: I = ∩ I_p for ideals in an integral domain. Mathlib has the ideal version for Dedekind domains (iInf_localization_eq_bot). The general integral domain version may need proof.

mathlib_declarations.name: IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum.iInf_localization_eq_bot
mathlib_declarations.description: Intersection of localizations of ideals in Dedekind domains

Lecture2/02_08_Example.notes: Z = ∩ Z_(p) in Q. This is a concrete instance of Corollary 2.7. Needs assembly from localization infrastructure.

mathlib_declarations.name: Localization.AtPrime
mathlib_declarations.description: Z_(p) construction

Lecture2/02_08a_Discussion.notes: Motivational discussion connecting localization principle to Dedekind domains. No formalizable content beyond what's in adjacent items.

Lecture2/02_09_Proposition.notes: Proposition 2.9: Noetherian + integrally closed + dim ≤ 1 ↔ all localizations at nonzero primes are DVRs. This is exactly the Dedekind domain characterization in Mathlib.

mathlib_declarations.name: IsDedekindDomain
mathlib_declarations.description: Dedekind domain: noetherian, dim ≤ 1, integrally closed

mathlib_declarations.name: IsDedekindDomainDvr
mathlib_declarations.description: DVR characterization of Dedekind domains

mathlib_declarations.name: IsDedekindDomain.isDedekindDomainDvr
mathlib_declarations.description: IsDedekindDomain → IsDedekindDomainDvr

mathlib_declarations.name: IsDedekindDomainDvr.ring_dimensionLEOne
mathlib_declarations.description: DVR localizations → dimension ≤ 1

mathlib_declarations.name: IsIntegrallyClosed
mathlib_declarations.description: Integrally closed typeclass

mathlib_declarations.name: Ring.DimensionLEOne
mathlib_declarations.description: Krull dimension ≤ 1 typeclass

Lecture2/02_10_Definition.notes: Definition 2.10: Dedekind domain. Directly corresponds to Mathlib's IsDedekindDomain.

mathlib_declarations.name: IsDedekindDomain
mathlib_declarations.description: A Dedekind domain is a noetherian, integrally closed domain of dimension ≤ 1

Lecture2/02_11_Corollary.notes: Corollary 2.11: Every PID is a Dedekind domain. Directly in Mathlib.

mathlib_declarations.name: IsPrincipalIdealRing.isDedekindDomain
mathlib_declarations.description: Every PID is a Dedekind domain

Lecture2/02_12_Remark.notes: Remark 2.12 discusses PID/UFD/Dedekind relationships. The key result (Dedekind + UFD → PID) is in Mathlib. The counterexamples (k[x,y] not Dedekind, Z[√(-13)] not UFD) would need construction.

mathlib_declarations.name: IsPrincipalIdealRing.of_isDedekindDomain_of_uniqueFactorizationMonoid
mathlib_declarations.description: Dedekind domain + UFD → PID

mathlib_declarations.name: Zsqrtd
mathlib_declarations.description: Z[√d] for d : ℤ (for Z[√(-13)] example)

Lecture2/02_12a_Discussion.notes: Integral closure of Dedekind domain in finite separable extension is Dedekind. Directly in Mathlib.

mathlib_declarations.name: IsIntegralClosure.isDedekindDomain
mathlib_declarations.description: Integral closure of Dedekind domain in finite separable extension is Dedekind

mathlib_declarations.name: integralClosure
mathlib_declarations.description: Integral closure of R in A

Lecture2/02_12b_Discussion.notes: Section 2.5 setup: standing assumptions (A noetherian domain, K = Frac A). No formalizable content.

Lecture2/02_13_Definition.notes: Definition 2.13: Fractional ideal = finitely generated A-submodule of K. Mathlib's FractionalIdeal uses the aI ⊆ A characterization.

mathlib_declarations.name: FractionalIdeal
mathlib_declarations.description: Type of fractional ideals (A-submodules I of K with aI ⊆ A for some nonzero a)

Lecture2/02_13a_Discussion.notes: Discussion of fractional vs integral ideals. Terminological, no standalone formalizable content.

Lecture2/02_14_Lemma.notes: Lemma 2.14: I is f.g. A-submodule of K iff aI ⊆ A for some nonzero a. This is built into Mathlib's FractionalIdeal definition.

mathlib_declarations.name: FractionalIdeal
mathlib_declarations.description: Uses the aI ⊆ A characterization as its definition

mathlib_declarations.name: FractionalIdeal.isFractional
mathlib_declarations.description: Every fractional ideal satisfies the aI ⊆ A property

Lecture2/02_15_Remark.notes: Remark on alternative definition of fractional ideals for non-noetherian domains. Discussion only.

Lecture2/02_16_Corollary.notes: Corollary 2.16: Every fractional ideal = (1/a)I. Follows from Mathlib's FractionalIdeal.isFractional.

mathlib_declarations.name: FractionalIdeal.isFractional
mathlib_declarations.description: Existence of a with aI ⊆ A, equivalent to I = (1/a)(aI)

mathlib_declarations.name: FractionalIdeal.coeIdeal
mathlib_declarations.description: Embedding of ideals into fractional ideals

Lecture2/02_17_Definition.notes: Definition 2.17: Principal fractional ideal (x) = xA. Directly in Mathlib.

mathlib_declarations.name: FractionalIdeal.spanSingleton
mathlib_declarations.description: Principal fractional ideal (x) = span {x}

Lecture2/02_17a_Discussion.notes: Operations on fractional ideals: sum, product, quotient (colon ideal). All in Mathlib.

mathlib_declarations.name: FractionalIdeal.instAdd
mathlib_declarations.description: Sum of fractional ideals I + J

mathlib_declarations.name: FractionalIdeal.instMul
mathlib_declarations.description: Product of fractional ideals IJ

mathlib_declarations.name: FractionalIdeal.instDiv
mathlib_declarations.description: Division/quotient I / J (colon ideal)

Lecture2/02_18_Lemma.notes: Lemma 2.18: I ÷ J is a fractional ideal. Built into the return type of FractionalIdeal division in Mathlib.

mathlib_declarations.name: FractionalIdeal.div_of_ne_zero
mathlib_declarations.description: Division of fractional ideals returns a fractional ideal

Lecture2/02_19_Definition.notes: Definition 2.19: Invertible fractional ideal (IJ = A). Mathlib has Inv instance and mul_inv_cancel for Dedekind domains.

mathlib_declarations.name: FractionalIdeal.mul_inv_cancel
mathlib_declarations.description: I * I⁻¹ = 1 for nonzero fractional ideals in Dedekind domains

Lecture2/02_20_Lemma.notes: Lemma 2.20: I invertible iff I(A ÷ I) = A, with I⁻¹ = A ÷ I. In Mathlib as inv_eq.

mathlib_declarations.name: FractionalIdeal.inv_eq
mathlib_declarations.description: I⁻¹ = 1 / I (i.e., A ÷ I)

Lecture2/02_20a_Discussion.notes: Preview that all nonzero fractional ideals of Dedekind domains are invertible. Discussion only.

Lecture2/02_21_Example.notes: Example 2.21: A = Z + 2iZ, I = 2Z[i] is not invertible as A-ideal. This specific example needs custom construction. Mathlib has GaussianInt (Z[i]) but not the subring Z + 2iZ.

mathlib_declarations.name: GaussianInt
mathlib_declarations.description: Z[i] (Gaussian integers)

Lecture2/02_21a_Discussion.notes: Fractional ideals form abelian monoid; invertible ones form abelian group. The group structure is in Mathlib.

mathlib_declarations.name: FractionalIdeal.instCommMonoid
mathlib_declarations.description: Commutative monoid structure on fractional ideals

Lecture2/02_22_Definition.notes: Definition 2.22: Ideal group I_A = group of invertible fractional ideals. In Mathlib as the units of FractionalIdeal.

mathlib_declarations.name: (FractionalIdeal (nonZeroDivisors A) K)ˣ
mathlib_declarations.description: Units of fractional ideals = group of invertible fractional ideals

Lecture2/02_22a_Discussion.notes: Principal fractional ideals P_A form subgroup. In Mathlib as toPrincipalIdeal.

mathlib_declarations.name: toPrincipalIdeal
mathlib_declarations.description: Homomorphism from K× to fractional ideal group (principal ideals)

Lecture2/02_23_Definition.notes: Definition 2.23: Ideal class group cl(A) = I_A / P_A. Directly in Mathlib as ClassGroup.

mathlib_declarations.name: ClassGroup
mathlib_declarations.description: The ideal class group (quotient of invertible fractional ideals by principal ones)

mathlib_declarations.name: ClassGroup.equiv
mathlib_declarations.description: ClassGroup R ≃* (FractionalIdeal)ˣ ⧸ (toPrincipalIdeal R K).range

Lecture2/02_24_Example.notes: Example 2.24: DVR has trivial class group. The general framework exists; the specific DVR class group triviality result needs assembly.

mathlib_declarations.name: IsDiscreteValuationRing
mathlib_declarations.description: DVR typeclass

mathlib_declarations.name: ClassGroup
mathlib_declarations.description: Class group for the triviality statement

mathlib_declarations.name: ClassGroup.mk0_eq_one_iff
mathlib_declarations.description: ClassGroup.mk0 I = 1 ↔ I is principal (for Dedekind domains)

Lecture2/02_25_Remark.notes: Remark 2.25: Dedekind domain is UFD iff cl(A) trivial. Mathlib has Dedekind + UFD → PID and the class group machinery, but the exact biconditional may need assembly.

mathlib_declarations.name: IsPrincipalIdealRing.of_isDedekindDomain_of_uniqueFactorizationMonoid
mathlib_declarations.description: Dedekind + UFD → PID

mathlib_declarations.name: ClassGroup.mk0_eq_one_iff
mathlib_declarations.description: Relates class group triviality to principalness of ideals

Backmatter/Bibliography.notes: Bibliography listing references. No formalizable content.

ext_commutative_rings.notes: CommRing, RingHom, Ideal, IsPrime, IsMaximal, quotient rings all in Mathlib.

ext_integral_domains.notes: IsDomain, FractionRing, fraction field construction all in Mathlib.

ext_noetherian_rings.notes: IsNoetherianRing and basic properties fully covered.

ext_pid.notes: IsPrincipalIdealRing, PID → UFD, PID → Dedekind all in Mathlib.

ext_ufd.notes: UniqueFactorizationMonoid and its properties in Mathlib.

ext_local_rings.notes: IsLocalRing, maximalIdeal, localization at prime gives local ring.

ext_krull_dimension.notes: Krull dimension via Order.krullDim and ringKrullDim. Ring.DimensionLEOne for dim ≤ 1.

ext_integral_closure.notes: integralClosure, IsIntegrallyClosed, IsIntegralClosure all in Mathlib.

ext_modules.notes: Module, Submodule, Submodule.FG, module homomorphisms all in Mathlib.

ext_tensor_products.notes: TensorProduct and base change in Mathlib.

ext_field_extensions.notes: Algebra.IsAlgebraic, Algebra.IsSeparable, FiniteDimensional all in Mathlib.

ext_polynomial_rings.notes: Polynomial rings and fraction fields of polynomial rings in Mathlib.

ext_gaussian_integers.notes: GaussianInt (Z[i]) in Mathlib. The subring Z + 2iZ used in Example 2.21 is not directly available.

ext_finite_fields.notes: ZMod p, GaloisField, and finite field infrastructure in Mathlib.

ext_dvr_lecture1.notes: DVR definition, uniformizers, valuations, residue fields all in Mathlib.

ext_theorem_1_16.notes: Theorem 1.16 (integrally closed noetherian local dim 1 → DVR) is the Dedekind domain DVR characterization in Mathlib.

ext_number_fields_lecture1.notes: Number fields and rings of integers in Mathlib.

ext_global_fields_lecture1.notes: Number fields covered. Global function fields partially covered (FunctionField exists in Mathlib but less developed).

ext_contraction_extension_props.notes: b^{ce} = b for localizations is IsLocalization.map_comap.

ext_prime_ideal_correspondence_proof.notes: The proof of the prime ideal correspondence is the content of IsLocalization.orderIsoOfPrime.

ext_z_sqrt_minus_13_not_ufd.notes: Z[√(-13)] is not a UFD. Zsqrtd exists but the non-UFD proof would need construction.

ext_k_xy_not_dedekind.notes: k[x,y] is a UFD but not Dedekind. Would need to show dim k[x,y] = 2. Mathlib has MvPolynomial but this specific result needs proof.

ext_dvr_integrally_closed.notes: DVR → integrally closed. IsDiscreteValuationRing implies IsDedekindDomain which implies IsIntegrallyClosed.

ext_invertible_ideals_abelian_group.notes: Group structure on invertible fractional ideals is the Units of FractionalIdeal in Mathlib.

coverage_summary.notes: The lecture content is very well covered by Mathlib. Core infrastructure (localization, Dedekind domains, fractional ideals, class groups) is fully available. Main gaps are specific examples (Z + 2iZ subring, Z[√(-13)] non-UFD, k[x,y] non-Dedekind) and the general submodule intersection result (Proposition 2.6). Most 'partially_covered' items just need assembly of existing Mathlib pieces rather than fundamentally new proofs.
